Lines.FullText: The coercion conditions are configured with an evaluation string when the coercer is constructed. The string constructs an

Elements.RawText: The coercion conditions are configured with an evaluation string when the coercer is constructed. The string constructs an

Lines.FullText: Eval value that replaces any instance of the keyword "value" or "val" with the value being set. The result of the Eval

Elements.RawText: Eval value that replaces any instance of the keyword "value" or "val" with the value being set. The result of the Eval

Lines.FullText: value evaluation is the output of the `coerce` function call. For example, coercers created with the string

Elements.RawText: value evaluation is the output of the `coerce` function call. For example, coercers created with the string

Lines.FullText: "if(value > 5, 5, value)" would enforce that the property value is always equal to or lower than 5.

Elements.RawText: "if(value > 5, 5, value)" would enforce that the property value is always equal to or lower than 5.

Tags.RawText:
*
 * The coercion conditions are configured with an evaluation string when the coercer is constructed. The string constructs an
 * Eval value that replaces any instance of the keyword "value" or "val" with the value being set. The result of the Eval
 * value evaluation is the output of the `coerce` function call. For example, coercers created with the string
 * "if(value > 5, 5, value)" would enforce that the property value is always equal to or lower than 5.

Lines.FullText: Whenever a value is set to on a Property object, if the corresponding Property has a coercer configured, the value will

Elements.RawText: Whenever a value is set to on a Property object, if the corresponding Property has a coercer configured, the value will

Lines.FullText: be evaluated and modified to fit the restrictions imposed by the coercer. For example, a coercer can enforce lower-than,

Elements.RawText: be evaluated and modified to fit the restrictions imposed by the coercer. For example, a coercer can enforce lower-than,

Lines.FullText: greater-than, equality, or other number relations on written values.

Elements.RawText: greater-than, equality, or other number relations on written values.

Description.RawText:
*
 * Whenever a value is set to on a Property object, if the corresponding Property has a coercer configured, the value will
 * be evaluated and modified to fit the restrictions imposed by the coercer. For example, a coercer can enforce lower-than,
 * greater-than, equality, or other number relations on written values.
